zh.Paris, Île-de-France, France: 法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲
zh.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre於1105年出生在法國。她於大約1119年與Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1175年在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年70歲。

zh-hans.Paris, Île-de-France, France: 法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre于1105年出生在法国。她于大约1119年与Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1175年在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre was born in 1105, in France. She married Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry about 1119. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1175, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre est née en 1105, à France. Elle a épousé Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry environ 1119. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1175, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre wurde 1105 in Frankreich geboren. Sie hat um 1119 Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1175 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre è nata il nel 1105 nel Francia. Lei sposato Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry circa 1119. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1175, nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierreは1105年にフランスで生まれた。約1119年にGuillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1175年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre(은)는 1105년 프랑스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1119년 Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1175년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre nasceu em 1105, em França. Ela casou-se com Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry aproximadamente 1119. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1175, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre родилась в 1105 г. в местности Франция. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry около 1119 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1175 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de France - Dame le Liancourt Saint Pierre nació en 1105, en Francia. Se casó con Guillaume de Chaumont - Seigneur de Chaumont et de Quitry aproximadamente 1119. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1175, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 70 años.
